description: Which property sources may supply each of the six CNS-MPO inputs. Enforced mechanically: a property record whose calculator is not listed for its property is REJECTED (the property becomes a missing_input; the score becomes incomplete). Nothing is silently substituted or imputed.

rationale_source.quote: For the work herein, calculated physicochemical properties were obtained using standard commercial packages: Biobyte for ClogP calculations, ACD/Laboratories for ClogD at pH 7.4, and ACD/Laboratories for pKa. For calculation of TPSA, see ref (8).

conformance_levels.published_method: The exact calculator used by Wager et al. 2010.
conformance_levels.published_method_equivalent: A different implementation of the SAME published algorithm (e.g. Ertl TPSA), recorded with its own version.
conformance_levels.documented_deviation: A different calculator for a property the tool genuinely implements. ALLOWED but always surfaced per property; never silent.
conformance_levels.forbidden: The tool does not implement this quantity at all. Using it would be fabrication.

allowed.note: Measured logP is not ClogP; flagged.
RDKit implements the Crippen/Wildman logP estimator. It is NOT BioByte ClogP; substituting it changes the score and must be visible.

forbidden.reason: RDKit does not implement a pH-dependent distribution coefficient (logD 7.4). There is no RDKit ClogD; any such value would be fabricated or a silent logP substitution.
RDKit does not implement pKa prediction. Any 'RDKit pKa' would be fabricated.

allowed.note: Average molecular weight from the molecular formula — a deterministic quantity RDKit genuinely implements.

mw.note: MW must be the MW of the ACTIVE MOIETY, not of a salt form. Salt-form MW is a common silent error; the firewall requires explicit salt->active-moiety mapping.

allowed.note: RDKit's TPSA implements the Ertl topological PSA method, which is the method Wager et al. cite (their ref 8).

hbd.note: HBD definitions differ between tools (Lipinski N/O-H count vs. explicit donor SMARTS). The definition used is recorded in the property record's 'method' field.

cross_candidate_rule.statement: If two candidates in the same scorecard set take the same property from different calculators, the set is flagged calculator_mixed_across_candidates=true, the affected property/calculator pairs are enumerated, and comparability across candidates for that property is marked not_comparable. The scores are still emitted (they are per-candidate valid) but the cross-candidate comparison is explicitly disclaimed.

hard_rules: No imputation. A missing, non-finite or disallowed input makes the CNS-MPO status 'incomplete' and the total null.
Every property record must carry: value, units, experimental|predicted, calculator id, method, software/database version, access date, source URL/record id, raw response sha256.
A property value with no source_record binding is rejected at the firewall.
